controls.exact-set: Identity-bearing collections are complete, unique, ordered where meaningful, and validated before normalization.
controls.identity-binding: Published identifiers, versions, paths, and source identities are verified against authoritative source documents.
controls.semantic-integrity: Protected semantics are validated completely rather than by prefixes, subsets, or self-consistency alone.
controls.canonical-manifest: All consumer-relevant identity metadata and required artifacts are bound by canonical digest semantics.
controls.evidence-provenance: Evidence names an exact source state reachable from the reviewed head and retains reproducible commands and results.
controls.reproducible-execution: Dependencies and bootstrap instructions are declared and clean-checkout replay is supported.
controls.exact-head-certification: CI certifies the submitted head explicitly and records any synthetic merge state separately.
controls.lifecycle-consistency: PR, taskchain, punchlist, changelog, release, and deploy states do not contradict one another.
controls.authority-boundary: The change cannot acquire authority beyond its declared product and execution boundary.
controls.negative-testing: Adversarial and malformed cases fail closed for every claimed invariant.
controls.rollback: A tested reversal or safe disable path exists for the declared lifecycle and authority level.
controls.truthfulness: Implemented, tested, proposed, accepted, released, and deployed claims are explicitly distinguished.
